data.title: What brands of forklifts do you sell?

children.text: We sell new and used forklifts from all major brands including Toyota, Hyster, Yale, Crown, Caterpillar, Komatsu, Mitsubishi, and more. Our equipment specialists can help you find the right brand and model for your specific needs.

Yes, we offer flexible financing options for both new and used equipment purchases. We work with multiple lenders to find competitive rates and terms that fit your budget. Contact our sales team to discuss financing options.

data.title: Can I trade in my old forklift?

children.text: Absolutely! We accept trade-ins on most makes and models. Our team will evaluate your current equipment and provide a fair trade-in value that can be applied toward your new purchase.

Rental rates vary based on the type of equipment, rental duration, and specific requirements. We offer daily, weekly, and monthly rates with discounts for longer-term rentals. Contact us for a customized quote.
Yes, we provide delivery and pickup service for all rental equipment throughout the Houston metro area and surrounding regions. Delivery charges may apply based on distance.

data.title: Do you offer emergency repair services?

children.text: Yes, we offer 24/7 emergency repair services. Our mobile technicians can be dispatched to your location to minimize equipment downtime. Call our emergency line at (832) 400-4679 for immediate assistance.

data.title: How often should I service my forklift?

children.text: We recommend servicing your forklift every 200-250 operating hours, or at least every three months. Regular maintenance extends equipment life, improves safety, and prevents costly breakdowns.
Yes, all of our training programs are fully OSHA-compliant and meet 29 CFR 1910.178 standards. Upon completion, operators receive an official certification card valid for three years.

Absolutely! We offer on-site training at your facility for maximum convenience. Our instructors will work with your team using your specific equipment and in your actual work environment for the most relevant training experience.
Texas Equipment Source has been our go-to for forklift maintenance and parts for over 5 years. Their response time is excellent and their technicians really know their stuff. Highly recommend!
We purchased three forklifts from TES and the experience was outstanding. They helped us find the right equipment for our warehouse and the delivery was right on schedule. Great team to work with.

The OSHA training program was thorough and professional. Our operators are now properly certified and more confident in their daily operations. The bilingual instruction was a huge plus for our diverse team.

data.title: 5 Essential Forklift Maintenance Tips to Extend Equipment Life
data.excerpt: Regular maintenance is key to keeping your forklifts running efficiently and safely. Here are five essential tips every operator and fleet manager should know.

children.text: Proper forklift maintenance is not just about preventing breakdowns — it's about ensuring the safety of your operators and the efficiency of your operations. Here are five maintenance tips that can significantly extend the life of your material handling equipment.
1. Check Fluids Regularly

Engine oil, hydraulic fluid, coolant, and transmission fluid should be checked daily as part of your pre-shift inspection. Low fluid levels can lead to overheating, component failure, and costly repairs.
2. Inspect Tires and Wheels
Worn or damaged tires affect stability and can be a safety hazard. Check for chunking, flat spots, and proper inflation on pneumatic tires. Replace tires when tread is worn to the wear line.
3. Keep the Mast and Forks Clean

Debris on the mast rails can cause premature wear and jerky operation. Regularly clean and grease the mast channels. Inspect forks for cracks, bends, and wear — replace if fork thickness is reduced by more than 10%.

data.title: How to Choose the Right Forklift for Your Business
data.excerpt: With so many options available, selecting the right forklift can be overwhelming. This guide breaks down the key factors to consider when making your decision.

children.text: Choosing the right forklift for your business involves more than just picking the first available option. The right equipment can improve productivity, reduce operating costs, and create a safer work environment. Here's what to consider.
Assess Your Application

Consider where the forklift will primarily operate. Indoor warehouse operations typically need electric forklifts with cushion tires, while outdoor use on rough terrain requires IC-powered units with pneumatic tires.
